qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Path traversal in V1 Files API allows arbitrary file read and delete
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding concerns V1 Files API path traversal through file_name into LocalStorageService path construction, not the advisory's API key deletion endpoint or missing API key ownership check.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unauthenticated remote code execution via default auto-login and custom component exec()
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding concerns default auto-login plus arbitrary Python execution in the custom_component endpoint, not delete_api_key_route(), delete_api_key(), or API key owner authorization.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code or root cause. The advisory is an IDOR in src/backend/base/langflow/api/v1/api_key.py where delete_api_key_route() deletes an api_key_id through delete_api_key() without verifying ownership. The qualifying findings instead report file path traversal in the V1 Files API and unauthenticated custom-component RCE via auto_login.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-rf6x-r45m-xv3w locates the root cause at src/backend/base/langflow/api/v1/api_key.py:44-53 and crud.py:44-49: authenticated API key deletion lacks an api_key.user_id == current_user.id check.

path_traversal_v1_files_api/vulnerability_report.json lists affected entrypoints under /api/v1/files and LocalStorageService path concatenation; it does not reference API key deletion or ownership verification.

autologin_rce_via_custom_component/vulnerability_report.json lists /api/v1/auto_login and /api/v1/custom_component with exec() of supplied code; it does not reference API key deletion or ownership verification.
